word: G2113 - εὐθυδρομέω - euthydroméō - yoo-thoo-drom-eh'-o - With a straight course

sections.paragraph:
- Original: εὐθυδρομέω

- Transliteration: Euthudromeo

- Phonetic: yoo-thoo-drom-eh'-o

- Definition:

1. to make a straight course, run a straight course

- Origin: from G2117 and G1408

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G2117 and G1408; to lay a straight course that is sail direct: - (come) with a straight course.
